content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: I'm implementing multi-file support for click.edit(); I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. What files does your feature touch?

fields.note: I'm implementing multi-file support for click.edit(); I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. What files does your feature touch?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Genuine overlap confirmed, both need same 2 files. Proposing region split instead: (1) termui.py edit(): I add 'filename: str | Iterable[str] | None' + overloads; you append 'process_priority' as a new trailing kwarg + pass-through to Editor(...) -- keep your diff hunk separate from mine (different param, different line). (2) _termui_impl.py Editor.__init__: you own entirely (add process_priority attr there), I won't touch __init__. (3) _termui_impl.py edit_file->edit_files body: I own the rename + signature change + building/joining the multi-file command string (the 'c = subprocess.Popen(...)' line construction). You apply priority AFTER that Popen call returns (e.g. right after 'c = subprocess.Popen(...)', before 'exit_code = c.wait()', using c.pid) rather than editing the Popen call line itself -- that keeps our hunks non-overlapping. My method will be named edit_files(self, filenames) and the Popen result var stays named 'c'. Does this work for you?

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Multi-file edit support complete and committed to patch.txt. Summary: Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) renamed to Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) in _termui_impl.py -- builds 'exec_filenames' by joining quoted filenames, then 'c = subprocess.Popen(f"{editor} {exec_filenames}", env=environ, shell=True)' (var still named 'c', right before 'exit_code = c.wait()' -- your priority hook goes there). Editor.__init__ untouched by me. In termui.py, edit() now takes filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, with two @t.overload variants above the impl; body normalizes to a tuple and calls ed.edit_files(filenames). Safe for you to re-read both files now and layer process_priority on top.
